body.title: Add LEVER memory type with high base confidence
body.result: MemoryType.LEVER added with BASE_CONFIDENCE=0.85, CLASS scope. 4 unit tests pass. Commit 40a0f070.
body.intention: I intend that a LEVER memory type exists for cross-domain causal/systemic patterns — 'why does this work?' knowledge — with high base confidence so it resists drift and surfaces reliably in pre-inference assembly.
body.description:
Add `LEVER = 'lever'` to the `MemoryType` enum and `BASE_CONFIDENCE[MemoryType.LEVER] = 0.85` (same tier as IDENTITY — cross-domain causal patterns are identity-level durable). LEVER is the memory type for knowledge that answers 'why does this work?' and transfers across domains (e.g. physics intuition that applies to software architecture, systems-thinking patterns that apply across Akien's domains).

Update `_INSTANCE_SCOPE_TYPES` to include LEVER in class scope (lever memories are cross-instance knowledge, not per-session).

**Affected files:** devices/igor/memory/models.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN — enum entry + BASE_CONFIDENCE entry + scope classification. OUT — categorizer wiring (separate ticket), source seeding (T-memory-source-seed).
**Completion criteria:** `from devices.igor.memory.models import MemoryType; MemoryType.LEVER` imports without error; `BASE_CONFIDENCE[MemoryType.LEVER] == 0.85`; `Memory(narrative='test', memory_type=MemoryType.LEVER).confidence` returns >= 0.85; at least 2 unit tests cover the new type.
